Cannabis in Trinidad and Tobago.title: Cannabis in Trinidad and Tobago
Cannabis in Trinidad and Tobago.text: The manufacture, possession, and consumption of cannabis in Trinidad and Tobago is decriminalized. In December 2019, the government passed a bill to decriminalize the cultivation and possession of small quantities of cannabis. The bill implemented tiered penalties for possession of 30-60 grams and allowed cultivation of up to four plants per adult.

Cannabis in the Netherlands.title: Cannabis in the Netherlands
Cannabis in the Netherlands.text: Cannabis in the Netherlands is illegal, but is decriminalised for personal use for those aged 18 and over. Recreational consumption is tolerated, and it is available in coffeeshops. Cannabis products are only sold openly in certain local coffeeshops and possession of up to 5 grams for personal use is decriminalised. Other types of sales and transportation are not permitted. Though retail sales are tolerated, production, transportation, and bulk possession of marijuana outside of retail stores is illegal. Medical cannabis has been available by prescription at Dutch pharmacies since 2003.

Cannabis in Germany.title: Cannabis in Germany
Cannabis in Germany.text: Cannabis in Germany was legalised for recreational use by adults on 1 April 2024. Adults may possess limited amounts of cannabis and cultivate a small number of plants for personal use. From July 2024, non-profit cannabis social clubs also became legal. Licensed commercial sales were excluded from the initial legislation. Medical cannabis is available by prescription and covered by health insurance in qualifying cases.

Cannabis in Spain.title: Cannabis in Spain
Cannabis in Spain.text: The use of cannabis products is decriminalized for personal cultivation and use. Sale and importation of any quantity of cannabis is a criminal offence, punishable by jail time. Purchase, possession and consumption in a public place constitutes a civil infraction punishable by a fine and confiscation of the product. Consumption and cultivation by adults in a private space is legal. Cannabis clubs have operated in a legal grey area, but courts have curtailed broad club legality.

Cannabis in France.title: Cannabis in France
Cannabis in France.text: Cannabis in France remains illegal for personal use. Limited types of cannabis-derived products are permitted for medical uses.

Cannabis in Finland.title: Cannabis in Finland
Cannabis in Finland.text: Cannabis in Finland is illegal. Production, import, transport, sale, possession and use are criminalised. Most personal use offences are handled with summary fines rather than court proceedings. Possession with intent to redistribute is brought to court and penalties are harsher. Medical cannabis is available only to an extremely limited group of users.

Cannabis in Portugal.title: Cannabis in Portugal
Cannabis in Portugal.text: Cannabis in Portugal is decriminalized. Possession for personal use is treated as a civil matter rather than a criminal penalty for a first-time offense. Medical use of cannabis was legalized in 2018. Personal cultivation for medical use remains against the law.
